calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paula Goodwin for Burr Preston (Petitioner/Executor).

Ms. Goodwin stated that she gave notice to the previous Attorneys, that she has not heard from them and there are no objections. The Court made reference to the Unified Transfer to Minor's Act, raised the issue of placing the funds into a blocked account and stated that its concern is protecting the Minor. Ms. Goodwin stated that there is a specific provision in the will (Article Fifth) which gives to the Daughter as Custodian for the Minor, that she does not know why the provision is in there and that she did not draft the document. Ms. Goodwin also made reference to Probate Code 3300 (A parent who receives any money or property belonging to a minor under any provision of this part shall account to the minor for the money or other property when the minor reaches the age of majority.), stated that if the Estate does not close by August 31st, the estate will owe taxes and it may cost the estate over $10,000.00, that a blocked account is contrary to what the Testator wrote and made reference to probate Code 3905(b) (If a custodian is named, distribution shall be made to that person.). In addition, Ms. Goodwin stated that the blocked account would have to be in California and that she does not know how it would work in Australia. The First Report on Waiver of Account is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
